Container.Name: uup-dump-windows-iso
Container.Overview: Downloads and creates the latest Windows ISO using UUP dump. Skips the download if the same build already exists. Supports Windows 10, Windows 11, and Windows Server 2022. Optional web UI mode for interactive configuration.
Container.Description: Downloads and creates Windows ISO files from UUP dump.[br][br] [b]Auto mode (default):[/b] Set MODE=auto. On each run:[br] 1. Fetches the latest build number[br] 2. Checks if an ISO for that build already exists[br] 3. If yes: logs and exits immediately[br] 4. If no: downloads all Windows Update packages and converts them to an ISO[br][br] [b]Web UI mode:[/b] Set MODE=web and map port 8080. The container starts a web server at http://[IP]:8080 where you can select the Windows version, build, edition, language, ring, conversion options, and which Store apps to include — then trigger the build from the browser. Settings are saved to /config/settings.json and reused on the next run. Change ExtraParams to --restart=unless-stopped when using this mode.[br][br] ISOs are named {major}.{minor}.Vibranium-X64-{LANG}-{EDITION}_Updated.iso[br][br] Temp files (~30 GB) are stored under /work and removed automatically.[br][br] Logs: a rolling uup-dump.log plus one per-run log named YYYY-MM-DD_build_target_lang_edition.log are written to the Log Directory.

Config.Name: ISO Output Path
Config.Description: Where the finished ISO, checksum, and metadata files are saved.

Config.Name: Log Directory
Config.Description: Where uup-dump.log and per-run log files are written. Defaults to the same folder as the ISOs.

Config.Name: Config Directory
Config.Description: Stores settings.json (web UI configuration), current-build.log, and build-status.json. Required for web UI mode to persist settings across restarts.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the web configuration UI. Only active when MODE=web. Open http://<unraid-ip>:8080 to configure and trigger builds.

Config.Name: Mode
Config.Description: auto = build on container start (current behavior). web = start the configuration web UI instead; set a port mapping for port 8080 and change restart policy to unless-stopped.

Config.Name: Web UI Port (env)
Config.Description: HTTP port the web UI listens on inside the container. Change this if you also change the port mapping above.

Config.Name: Work Directory
Config.Description: Path inside the container used for temp build files (~30 GB). Default /work uses the container internal layer (recommended when Docker data is on your cache SSD). To use an external share instead: add a volume mapping e.g. /mnt/cache/uup-work -> /work-ext, then set this to /work-ext.

Config.Name: Windows Target
Config.Description: Which Windows version to build: windows-11, windows-10, or windows-2022. Overridden by web UI settings when MODE=web.

Config.Name: Windows Ring
Config.Description: RETAIL=current stable release. DEV=Dev Channel insider. BETA=Beta Channel. CANARY=Canary Channel. RP=Release Preview. ANY=newest build regardless of ring. Overridden by web UI settings when MODE=web.

Config.Name: Language
Config.Description: Language pack, e.g. de-de, en-us, fr-fr, es-es, it-it, pl-pl. Overridden by web UI settings when MODE=web.

Config.Name: Edition
Config.Description: Windows edition: Professional, Home, ServerStandard, ServerDatacenter. Overridden by web UI settings when MODE=web.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file ownership (Unraid default: nobody = 99)

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file ownership (Unraid default: users = 100)
